Title: Create a lab order for a single test from an active visit
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with Order Entry privilege; patient has an active visit; on the patient dashboard Orders widget
Test_Steps: 1. Open the Orders section | 2. Click Add lab order | 3. Search and select the lab test | 4. Set order urgency to Routine | 5. Enter clinical notes | 6. Click Save order
Test_Data: Test=Complete Blood Count; Urgency=Routine; Notes=Rule out anemia
Expected_Result: A new lab order is created in Ordered status, linked to the active visit, with the ordering provider, date/time, and an order number displayed in the active orders list

Title: Create a lab order with STAT urgency
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with Order Entry privilege; patient has an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open Orders | 2. Click Add lab order | 3. Select the lab test | 4. Set urgency to STAT | 5. Save order
Expected_Result: Order is saved with urgency STAT and is visually flagged/prioritized in the active orders list with the STAT indicator

Title: Reject lab order with no test selected
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with Order Entry privilege; patient has an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open Orders | 2. Click Add lab order | 3. Leave the test field empty | 4. Click Save order
Expected_Result: Order is not saved; a validation message indicates a test must be selected and focus returns to the test field

Title: Block lab order when patient has no active visit
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with Order Entry privilege; selected patient has NO active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to patient dashboard | 2. Attempt to open Add lab order
Expected_Result: System prevents order entry and prompts to start a visit first; no order is created

Title: Warn on duplicate active lab order for same test
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active lab order for Complete Blood Count in Ordered status
Test_Steps: 1. Open Orders | 2. Add lab order | 3. Select the same test already active | 4. Save order
Expected_Result: A duplicate-order warning is shown allowing override with reason or cancel; if overridden the second order is saved with the captured reason

Title: Decision table for urgency vs required fields
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with Order Entry privilege; active visit present
Test_Steps: 1. Add lab order | 2. Select test | 3. For each rule set urgency and scheduled date combination | 4. Attempt save | 5. Observe acceptance/rejection
Test_Data: R1 Routine+noDate=accept; R2 Scheduled+noDate=reject; R3 Scheduled+futureDate=accept; R4 STAT+pastDate=reject
Expected_Result: Each rule yields the expected accept or reject outcome; Scheduled urgency mandates a future scheduled date

Title: Order a panel that expands into component tests
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; active visit; lab panel set configured (Platform)
Test_Steps: 1. Add lab order | 2. Search and select the panel | 3. Save order | 4. Open the order detail
Expected_Result: Panel order is created and its component tests are represented as a grouped order set so results can be entered per component

Title: Edit clinical notes on an existing Ordered lab order (revise)
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; an existing lab order in Ordered status
Test_Steps: 1. Open active orders | 2. Select the order | 3. Choose Modify/Revise | 4. Update clinical notes | 5. Save
Expected_Result: A revision order is created linking to the previous order; the updated notes are reflected and history shows the prior version

Title: Cancel (discontinue) an Ordered lab order with reason
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; lab order exists in Ordered status
Test_Steps: 1. Open active orders | 2. Select the order | 3. Click Discontinue/Cancel | 4. Enter cancellation reason | 5. Confirm
Expected_Result: The order moves to a discontinued/cancelled state with the reason recorded and timestamp; it no longer appears in active orders

Title: Block cancellation of an already verified result order
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; lab order is in Verified status with a final result
Test_Steps: 1. Open the order | 2. Attempt to Discontinue/Cancel
Expected_Result: Cancellation is blocked or restricted with a message that a verified/resulted order cannot be cancelled; appropriate amendment path is suggested

Title: Record specimen collection for an Ordered lab test
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab/Phlebotomy staff; lab order in Ordered status (Platform LIS workflow)
Test_Steps: 1. Open the lab worklist | 2. Select the Ordered test | 3. Record specimen collection | 4. Enter specimen type and collection datetime | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Order transitions from Ordered to Collected; specimen identifier, type, and collection datetime are recorded and visible

Title: Reject specimen collection with future collection datetime
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab staff; lab order in Ordered status (Platform LIS workflow)
Test_Steps: 1. Open worklist | 2. Select test | 3. Enter a collection datetime in the future | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Collection is rejected with a validation message that collection datetime cannot be in the future

Title: Specimen rejection routes order back for recollection
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; specimen in Collected status (Platform LIS workflow)
Test_Steps: 1. Open the specimen record | 2. Mark specimen as Rejected | 3. Select rejection reason | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Specimen is flagged rejected with reason; the order returns to a state requiring recollection and notifies the ordering team

Title: Enter a numeric lab result within normal reference range
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; lab order in Collected/In-Progress status; reference ranges configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open the order in the lab worklist | 2. Enter the numeric result value | 3. Select units | 4. Save result
Expected_Result: Result is saved and order transitions to Resulted; value is displayed with units and shows as within normal range (not flagged)

Title: Enter a coded (qualitative) lab result
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; qualitative test order in In-Progress status
Test_Steps: 1. Open the order | 2. Select the coded result value from the answer set | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Coded result is saved with the selected concept answer; order transitions to Resulted and the qualitative value displays correctly

Title: Flag a result above the high reference range as abnormal High
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; reference range for Potassium configured (3.5-5.1 mmol/L)
Test_Steps: 1. Open the order | 2. Enter a value above the high limit | 3. Save result
Expected_Result: Result is saved and automatically flagged as High/Abnormal (above range); the abnormal flag is visible on the result and patient summary

Title: Flag a result below the low reference range as abnormal Low
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; reference range for Hemoglobin configured (13.0-17.0 g/dL male)
Test_Steps: 1. Open the order | 2. Enter a value below the low limit | 3. Save result
Expected_Result: Result is saved and flagged as Low/Abnormal (below range); abnormal indicator is displayed

Title: Boundary values exactly at low and high reference limits are normal
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; reference range 3.5-5.1 mmol/L configured for Potassium
Test_Steps: 1. Enter value equal to low limit and save | 2. Enter value equal to high limit on a second order and save | 3. Observe flags
Expected_Result: Both boundary values (3.5 and 5.1) are treated as within normal range and are NOT flagged abnormal

Title: Just-outside boundary values are flagged abnormal
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; reference range 3.5-5.1 mmol/L configured
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a value just below low and save | 2. Enter a value just above high and save | 3. Observe flags
Expected_Result: 3.4 is flagged Low and 5.2 is flagged High; both abnormal indicators appear

Title: Age- and sex-specific reference range selection
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; sex/age dependent ranges configured for Hemoglobin
Test_Steps: 1. Enter same Hemoglobin value for a male and a female patient | 2. Save each | 3. Compare flags applied
Test_Data: Value=13.5 g/dL; Male range 13.0-17.0; Female range 11.5-15.5
Expected_Result: The same value is normal for the male patient and may differ for the female patient based on the sex-specific range; correct range is applied per patient demographics

Title: Critical (panic) value triggers critical flag and alert
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; critical thresholds configured for Potassium (>6.0 critical high)
Test_Steps: 1. Open order | 2. Enter a critical-high value | 3. Save result
Expected_Result: Result is flagged Critical (panic) distinctly from routine abnormal; a critical-result alert/notification workflow is triggered for the ordering provider

Title: Critical result acknowledgement is captured for audit
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; a critical lab result is pending acknowledgement
Test_Steps: 1. Open notifications/inbox | 2. Open the critical result | 3. Acknowledge it | 4. Record action taken
Expected_Result: Acknowledgement is recorded with user, timestamp, and action; the audit trail reflects who acknowledged the critical value and when

Title: Reject non-numeric input for a numeric test
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; numeric test order in In-Progress status
Test_Steps: 1. Open the order | 2. Enter alphabetic text in the numeric result field | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Save is rejected with a validation error that the value must be numeric; no result is persisted

Title: Reject negative value for a test that cannot be negative
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; numeric test with configured absolute range (>=0)
Test_Steps: 1. Open the order | 2. Enter a negative value | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Negative value is rejected with a validation message indicating value is outside the allowed absolute range

Title: Reject value exceeding absolute/critical implausible maximum
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; absolute high limit configured for the test
Test_Steps: 1. Open the order | 2. Enter an implausibly high value above the absolute max | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Implausible value is rejected or requires confirmation; the system warns the value is outside the absolute allowable range

Title: Verify a resulted lab result by an authorized verifier
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Supervisor with verify privilege; order in Resulted status
Test_Steps: 1. Open the resulted order | 2. Review the entered value | 3. Click Verify/Approve | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Order transitions from Resulted to Verified; verifier identity and timestamp are recorded; result becomes final and visible to clinicians

Title: Prevent the same user from entering and verifying when segregation required
Preconditions: Result-entry user lacks verify privilege; segregation-of-duties policy enforced (Platform)
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as result-entry-only user | 2. Enter a result | 3. Attempt to Verify the same result
Expected_Result: Verify action is unavailable or denied for the entering user; verification requires a separate authorized verifier

Title: Amend a verified result creates a corrected version with audit
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Supervisor; order in Verified status
Test_Steps: 1. Open the verified result | 2. Choose Amend/Correct | 3. Enter the corrected value and reason | 4. Save
Expected_Result: A corrected result version is created (original preserved); amendment reason, user, and timestamp are recorded and the corrected value is marked as amended
Healthcare_Classification: Clinical; Patient Safety; Audit; Data Integrity

Title: Full happy-path state transition Ordered to Verified
Preconditions: Logged in with appropriate roles across steps; patient active visit; LIS workflow enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Create lab order (Ordered) | 2. Record specimen collection (Collected) | 3. Start processing (In-Progress) | 4. Enter result (Resulted) | 5. Verify result (Verified)
Expected_Result: Order progresses Ordered to Collected to In-Progress to Resulted to Verified; each transition records actor and timestamp; final state is Verified
Healthcare_Classification: Clinical; Patient Safety; Audit; Data Integrity

Title: Reject invalid transition from Ordered directly to Resulted
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; order in Ordered status; LIS enforces collection before result
Test_Steps: 1. Open an Ordered order | 2. Attempt to enter a result without recording collection
Expected_Result: System blocks result entry until specimen collection is recorded, enforcing the valid state sequence

Title: Cannot revert a Verified order back to In-Progress
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Supervisor; order in Verified status
Test_Steps: 1. Open the verified order | 2. Attempt to change status back to In-Progress
Expected_Result: Reverse transition is not permitted; only an amendment workflow is allowed, preserving audit integrity

Title: Create a radiology order selecting an imaging modality
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with Order Entry privilege; active visit; radiology orderables configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open Orders | 2. Click Add radiology/imaging order | 3. Select the imaging study | 4. Select modality | 5. Enter reason for exam | 6. Save
Test_Data: Study=Chest X-Ray; Modality=XR; Reason=Cough x 2 weeks
Expected_Result: Radiology order is created in Ordered status with selected modality and clinical reason; appears in the imaging worklist

Title: Capture contrast and laterality on a radiology order
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; active visit; CT orderable with contrast/laterality options (Platform)
Test_Steps: 1. Add radiology order | 2. Select CT study | 3. Set contrast = With contrast | 4. Set laterality = Left | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Order saves with contrast and laterality attributes captured and displayed on the order and worklist entry

Title: Pairwise coverage of modality, urgency, contrast, portable
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; active visit; multiple modalities configured (Platform)
Test_Steps: 1. Execute each pairwise combination of modality x urgency x contrast x portable | 2. Save each order | 3. Verify attributes persisted
Test_Data: P1 XR/STAT/None/Portable; P2 CT/Routine/With/Non-portable; P3 MRI/Scheduled/With/Non-portable; P4 US/Routine/None/Portable
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination saves successfully with the chosen attributes persisted; no invalid combination is silently accepted

Title: Safety prompt for MRI order with documented metal implant
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has documented ferromagnetic implant; MRI safety screening enabled (Platform)
Test_Steps: 1. Add radiology order | 2. Select MRI study | 3. Save | 4. Observe safety screening prompt
Expected_Result: An MRI safety/contraindication warning is displayed requiring screening acknowledgement before the order can proceed

Title: Link a radiology report to its imaging order
Preconditions: Logged in as Radiologist; radiology order in In-Progress with completed acquisition (Platform/PACS)
Test_Steps: 1. Open the imaging worklist | 2. Select the study | 3. Enter findings and impression | 4. Finalize report | 5. Verify linkage
Test_Data: Study=Chest X-Ray; Impression=No acute cardiopulmonary process
Expected_Result: Report is saved and linked to the originating order; clinicians can open the report from the order; order transitions to Resulted/Reported

Title: Critical imaging finding triggers notification
Preconditions: Logged in as Radiologist; critical finding workflow enabled (Platform)
Test_Steps: 1. Open the study | 2. Mark finding as Critical | 3. Enter critical finding text | 4. Finalize | 5. Confirm notification
Expected_Result: Critical imaging finding is flagged and a notification/communication is dispatched to the ordering provider with acknowledgement tracking

Title: Reject radiology order without reason for exam
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; active visit; reason-for-exam mandatory (Platform)
Test_Steps: 1. Add radiology order | 2. Select study and modality | 3. Leave reason for exam empty | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Order is rejected with a validation message that reason for exam is required for imaging orders

Title: Create a lab order via REST API
Preconditions: Valid API session/token; patient UUID and orderable concept UUID known; OpenMRS REST module available
Test_Steps: 1. POST to the order endpoint with type=testorder, patient, concept, orderer, careSetting | 2. Read response status and body | 3. GET the created order by UUID
Test_Data: patient=UUID; concept=CBC UUID; orderer=provider UUID; careSetting=OUTPATIENT
Expected_Result: API returns 201 with the created order UUID and Ordered status; subsequent GET returns the persisted order matching the request

Title: Reject API order creation with missing required field
Preconditions: Valid API token; OpenMRS REST module available
Test_Steps: 1. POST an order payload omitting the concept field | 2. Inspect HTTP status and error body
Expected_Result: API returns a 4xx validation error identifying the missing required field; no order is created

Title: Reject order API request with invalid/expired token
Preconditions: Expired or invalid authentication token
Test_Steps: 1. Send POST to the order endpoint with an invalid token | 2. Inspect HTTP status
Expected_Result: API returns 401 Unauthorized; no order is created and no PHI is leaked in the error response

Title: Authorization check prevents low-privilege user creating orders
Preconditions: Authenticated user lacking Order Entry privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as a user without order privileges | 2. POST a valid order payload | 3. Inspect status
Expected_Result: API returns 403 Forbidden; order is not created; attempt is auditable

Title: Retrieve a lab order as a FHIR ServiceRequest
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint available; an existing lab order; valid auth
Test_Steps: 1. GET ServiceRequest by id or search by patient | 2. Validate resourceType and key fields | 3. Validate status and intent
Expected_Result: Response is a valid FHIR R4 ServiceRequest with correct subject reference, status (active/order), intent=order, and code; passes FHIR validation

Title: Lab result exposed as FHIR Observation with LOINC and reference range
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint; verified lab result exists; LOINC mapping configured
Test_Steps: 1. GET Observation for the result | 2. Validate code uses LOINC system | 3. Validate valueQuantity with UCUM units | 4. Validate referenceRange and interpretation
Expected_Result: Observation contains LOINC code with system http://loinc.org, valueQuantity in UCUM units, referenceRange, and interpretation code (e.g., N/H/L) consistent with the flag

Title: DiagnosticReport links results to the originating ServiceRequest
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint; verified lab panel with results; basedOn linkage supported
Test_Steps: 1. GET DiagnosticReport | 2. Validate result references to Observations | 3. Validate basedOn reference to ServiceRequest
Expected_Result: DiagnosticReport correctly references its Observation results and links basedOn to the originating ServiceRequest; status reflects final

Title: Radiology order as ServiceRequest with imaging category
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint; an imaging order exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET the ServiceRequest for the imaging order | 2. Validate category=Imaging | 3. Validate code (e.g., RadLex/LOINC/SNOMED)
Expected_Result: ServiceRequest reflects imaging category with a valid procedure code and correct subject/requester references

Title: Inbound HL7 v2 ORM order message creates/updates an order
Preconditions: HL7 v2 interface engine connected; LIS sends ORM^O01 (Platform integration)
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ORM^O01 message with ORC and OBR segments | 2. Confirm ACK received | 3. Verify order state in the system
Expected_Result: System returns an AA acknowledgement and the order is created/updated to match the OBR/ORC content

Title: Inbound HL7 v2 ORU result message posts results with abnormal flags
Preconditions: HL7 v2 interface engine connected; LIS sends ORU^R01 (Platform integration)
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ORU^R01 with OBX result segments | 2. Confirm ACK | 3. Verify the result and abnormal flag posted
Expected_Result: Result is posted to the matching order with the HL7 abnormal flag (OBX-8) mapped to the system abnormal/High indicator; ACK is AA
Healthcare_Classification: Standards-HL7; Clinical; Patient Safety; Data Integrity

Title: Malformed HL7 message is rejected with negative acknowledgement
Preconditions: HL7 v2 interface engine connected (Platform integration)
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ORU message with a missing required MSH field | 2. Inspect the acknowledgement
Expected_Result: Interface returns an AE/AR negative acknowledgement; no malformed result is persisted; error is logged

Title: Lab order persists correct foreign keys and status in the orders table
Preconditions: Database read access; a lab order created via the UI
Test_Steps: 1. Query the orders/test_order tables for the new order | 2. Verify patient_id, concept_id, orderer, care_setting | 3. Verify order_action and date_activated
Expected_Result: Persisted row contains correct patient, concept, orderer, and care setting foreign keys, action=NEW, and a non-null activation date matching the UI

Title: Discontinuing an order preserves the original row and adds linkage
Preconditions: Database read access; an order discontinued via the UI
Test_Steps: 1. Identify the original order row | 2. Identify the discontinuation order row | 3. Verify previous_order linkage and stop/auto-expire dates
Test_Data: original order UUID; discontinue reason recorded
Expected_Result: Original order row is retained (immutable history); the discontinuation order references the previous order via the linkage column with a recorded date

Title: Order creation and changes are captured in the audit log
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; access to audit/history records
Test_Steps: 1. Create a lab order | 2. Modify it | 3. Discontinue it | 4. Review the audit/history entries
Expected_Result: Each action is recorded with actor, timestamp, and change detail; the audit trail reconstructs the full lifecycle of the order

Title: Result view access restricted to authorized roles only
Preconditions: Two users: one with result-view privilege, one without
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as authorized user and open a result (allowed) | 2. Log in as unauthorized user and attempt to open the same result
Expected_Result: Authorized user views the result; unauthorized user is denied and no PHI is exposed; the denied access attempt is logged

Title: Lab order form is fully keyboard navigable
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; on the Add lab order form
Test_Steps: 1. Use Tab/Shift+Tab to traverse all fields | 2. Operate the test search and dropdowns via keyboard | 3. Submit using Enter | 4. Verify focus order
Expected_Result: All interactive controls are reachable and operable by keyboard in a logical order; visible focus indicators are present and the form submits without a mouse

Title: Abnormal/critical flags are not conveyed by color alone
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; results view with abnormal and critical flags present
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect abnormal and critical result indicators | 2. Verify text/icon/ARIA label accompanies color | 3. Check with a screen reader
Test_Data: Results with High, Low, and Critical flags
Expected_Result: Each abnormal/critical indicator includes a non-color cue (text/icon/ARIA label) so the status is perceivable without relying on color; screen reader announces the status

Title: Exploratory session on rapid concurrent order entry and editing
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; active visit; test environment
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly add multiple lab and radiology orders | 2. Edit and discontinue some mid-flow | 3. Switch patients quickly | 4. Observe UI consistency, duplicates, and state correctness
Test_Data: Mixed lab + radiology orders; rapid actions
Expected_Result: No orphaned, duplicated, or mismatched-patient orders occur; UI remains consistent and order states stay correct; any anomalies are documented

Title: Exploratory testing of unusual specimen and result values
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; In-Progress orders available
Test_Steps: 1. Enter edge values (zero, very large, many decimals, scientific notation) | 2. Enter unusual units | 3. Attempt special characters in comments | 4. Observe handling
Expected_Result: System handles or gracefully rejects edge inputs without crashing; rounding/units behavior is reasonable and consistent; no data corruption

Title: Allergy/contraindication interaction check on order placement
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has a recorded contrast allergy; contrast imaging order attempted (Platform)
Test_Steps: 1. Add a contrast-enhanced imaging order for the patient | 2. Save | 3. Observe interaction/allergy alert
Test_Data: Patient allergy=Iodinated contrast; Order=CT with contrast
Expected_Result: An allergy/contraindication alert is presented before the order is finalized, requiring acknowledgement or override with reason

Title: Cancelled order cannot receive a result
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; an order in Cancelled/Discontinued status
Test_Steps: 1. Open the cancelled order | 2. Attempt to record specimen or enter a result
Expected_Result: Result/specimen entry is blocked for cancelled orders with a clear message; no result is attached to a cancelled order

Title: Resulted lab value appears on patient clinical summary linked to order
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; a verified lab result exists for the patient
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient clinical summary/dashboard | 2. Locate the lab result | 3. Click through to the originating order
Expected_Result: The verified result is displayed on the patient summary and links back to its originating order with consistent value, units, and date

Title: Result without configured reference range shows no false abnormal flag
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; a test with no reference range configured
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a numeric result for the test | 2. Save | 3. Observe flagging
Expected_Result: Result is saved without an abnormal flag and indicates no reference range available; no misleading normal/abnormal indicator is shown

Title: Scheduled (future-dated) lab order is queued not active
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; active visit; scheduled orders supported
Test_Steps: 1. Add lab order | 2. Set urgency=On Scheduled Date | 3. Set a future scheduled date | 4. Save | 5. Check order state on current date
Expected_Result: Order is saved as scheduled/future and is not yet active for collection until the scheduled date; it is clearly marked as future-dated

Title: Cancel a radiology order before image acquisition
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; radiology order in Ordered status (not yet acquired)
Test_Steps: 1. Open the imaging order | 2. Click Cancel/Discontinue | 3. Enter reason | 4. Confirm
Test_Data: Study=Chest X-Ray; Reason=Clinically no longer indicated
Expected_Result: Order transitions to cancelled with reason and timestamp; it is removed from the active imaging worklist

Title: Reflex/dependent test auto-orders based on result rule
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Technician; reflex rule configured (e.g., positive screen triggers confirmatory test)
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a result that meets the reflex condition | 2. Save | 3. Observe auto-generated reflex order
Expected_Result: A reflex/confirmatory order is automatically generated and linked to the triggering result per the configured rule; if condition not met, no reflex order is created
